# Feature: Environment Import ‚Äî Postman Support + Environment Type Dropdown

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-006
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

Let users import Postman environment exports directly. Today `EnvImportWizard` ([EnvImportWizard.tsx](../../packages/core/src/components/common/EnvImportWizard.tsx)) accepts only Wave-shaped environment JSON and validates nothing beyond `JSON.parse` ‚Äî a Postman export imports as a broken environment or fails downstream. This feature adds a lightweight environment transformer module (mirroring the collection transformer pattern), an auto-detected **Environment Type** dropdown in the wizard (same UX as the collection import wizard), and converts Postman exports to Wave format **in the UI layer before** calling `onImportEnvironments` ‚Äî so the MessageHandler/server import path stays Wave-only, exactly how collection imports work.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No new persisted shapes ‚Äî output is always a Wave `Environment` per the FEAT-001 schema.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`EnvironmentImportFormatType`** (new union type): `'wave' | 'postman'`.
- **Postman environment export** (input shape, validated structurally):
  - `name` (string) ‚Äî required
  - `values` (array) ‚Äî required; each: `key` (string), `value` (string), `enabled` (boolean, default `true`), `type` (string, optional ‚Äî `'secret'` or `'default'`)
  - `_postman_variable_scope` (string, optional) ‚Äî `'environment'` is the strongest detection signal
  - `id`/`_postman_exported_at`/`_postman_exported_using` ‚Äî ignored (never trust Postman's `id`)
- **Mapping Postman ‚Üí Wave `Environment`**:
  - `id` ‚Üê freshly generated (`crypto.randomUUID()`/`generateUniqueId` ‚Äî do not trust Postman's)
  - `name` ‚Üê `name`
  - `version` ‚Üê `CURRENT_ENVIRONMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` (FEAT-001)
  - `values[]` ‚Üê `values[]`: `key` ‚Üê `key`, `value` ‚Üê `value`, `enabled` ‚Üê `enabled` (default `true` when absent), `type` ‚Üê `'secret'` when Postman `type === 'secret'`, else `'default'`
- **Wave format** (single env object or array of envs): validated by FEAT-001's `validateWaveEnvironment`.

### Relationships

- Detection signals (content-based, mirroring FEAT-005's `detectFormatFromContent`): `_postman_variable_scope === 'environment'` **or** `values` is an array of `{key, value}` objects without a Wave `values[].type`/`id` shape ‚Üí `'postman'`; objects/arrays passing (or near) the Wave shape ‚Üí `'wave'`; else `undefined`.

### Indexes & Constraints

- Import boundary stays Wave-only: `IStorageAdapter.importEnvironments(fileContent)` receives Wave-format JSON; `EnvironmentService.import` (with FEAT-001 validation) is unchanged by this feature.

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/utils/transformers/environmentTransformers.ts` ‚Äî NEW: types, Postman‚ÜíWave transform, detection, `transformEnvironments` entry point
- `packages/core/src/utils/transformers/index.ts` ‚Äî re-export the environment transformer API
- `packages/core/src/components/common/EnvImportWizard.tsx` ‚Äî Environment Type dropdown, content detection, transform-before-import
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/transformers/environmentTransformers.test.ts` ‚Äî NEW: fixture-based unit tests
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/EnvImportWizard.test.tsx` ‚Äî wizard tests (extend or create)
- `docs/features/environments.md` ‚Äî document Postman import support

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001** (Wave Schema Versioning): `validateWaveEnvironment` + `CURRENT_ENVIRONMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` are used for wave-format validation and version stamping. Hard dependency ‚Äî implement FEAT-001 first.
- **FEAT-005** establishes the `detectFormatFromContent` pattern this module mirrors (soft dependency ‚Äî pattern reference only).

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Environment transformer module (`environmentTransformers.ts`)

- [ ] **Implementation**: Create `packages/core/src/utils/transformers/environmentTransformers.ts` exporting:
  - `type EnvironmentImportFormatType = 'wave' | 'postman'` and `ENVIRONMENT_IMPORT_FORMAT_OPTIONS` (label/value pairs for the dropdown, mirroring `IMPORT_FORMAT_OPTIONS`).
  - `detectEnvironmentFormat(text: string): EnvironmentImportFormatType | undefined` ‚Äî parse JSON (try/catch); Postman signals: `_postman_variable_scope === 'environment'`, or `values` array of `{key, value}` lacking Wave's `enabled`+`type` field shape; Wave signals: single object or array with `name` + `values[]` of `{key, value, type, enabled}`; else `undefined`.
  - `transformEnvironments(text: string, format: EnvironmentImportFormatType): Result<Environment[], string>` ‚Äî `wave`: parse (single or array) and validate each via FEAT-001 `validateWaveEnvironment` (after stamping `version`/missing fields is **not** done here ‚Äî wave files must already be valid; stamping stays in the service); `postman`: structural validation (name + values array) then map per ¬ß2 (fresh `id`, stamped `version`, secret-type mapping, `enabled` default true), returning a one-element array. Use `unknown` + narrowing, never `any` (memory).
  Re-export from `transformers/index.ts`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: fixture-based ‚Äî Postman fixture (mixed `enabled` flags, one `type: 'secret'`, one missing `type`) ‚Üí Wave envs with correct `type`/`enabled`/fresh id/version; Postman without `_postman_variable_scope` but with `{key,value}` values detected as postman; Wave single object and Wave array both detected and passed through validated; malformed JSON ‚Üí `err`; Postman missing `values` ‚Üí descriptive `err`; garbage object ‚Üí detection `undefined`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on all exports with an `@example` of the Postman mapping.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- environmentTransformers` green.

### TASK-002: `EnvImportWizard` ‚Äî Environment Type dropdown + transform before import

- [ ] **Implementation**: Mirror the collection wizard UX:
  - On file select, read `file.text()` immediately, cache in state, set an `environmentType` state from `detectEnvironmentFormat(text) ?? 'wave'`.
  - Add an **Environment Type** dropdown (same component/styling as the collection wizard's type dropdown; options from `ENVIRONMENT_IMPORT_FORMAT_OPTIONS`), manually overridable; helper text `Detected from file content`.
  - In `handleImport`: run `transformEnvironments(cachedText, environmentType)`; on `err` show the message inline (existing error banner); on `ok` call `onImportEnvironments(fileName, JSON.stringify(envs))` ‚Äî always Wave-format JSON downstream. Drop the JSON-extension hard requirement? **No** ‚Äî keep `.json`-only (both formats are JSON; unchanged behavior).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: RTL ‚Äî selecting a Postman export preselects "Postman"; user override to Wave then importing a Postman file surfaces the validation error inline; successful Postman import calls `onImportEnvironments` with Wave-shaped JSON (parse the arg and assert `values[0].type === 'secret'` mapping); Wave file imports unchanged; non-JSON file still rejected.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: `docs/features/environments.md` ‚Äî import section: supported formats (Wave JSON single/array, Postman environment export), note that secrets-typed Postman variables map to Wave `secret` variables.
- **Verify**: Export an environment from Postman, import it in either app ‚Äî variables appear with correct enabled/secret states; the persisted JSON on disk is Wave-shaped with a fresh `id` and `version: "0.0.1"`.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: Transformer returns `Result` (memory pattern); wizard shows `err` inline via the existing banner; downstream `EnvironmentService.import` (FEAT-001) is the validation backstop for the Wave JSON handed over.
- **Logging**: Never log environment file contents or variable values ‚Äî Postman exports may contain plaintext secrets (plan security note). Errors logged by the service layer carry env name/id only.
- **Security**: Postman variables with `type === 'secret'` map to Wave `type: 'secret'` and follow the existing secret-variable handling.
